title: Sticky Aubergine Rice Bowl
summary: Sticky, sweet and spicy - this aubergine rice bowl’s packed with veg, full of flavour and makes a top-tier lunch box.

ingredients.label: 2 Tbsp Sesame Seeds, Plus Extra To Serve
ingredients.name: Sesame Seeds

ingredients.label: 1 Tbsp Agave Nectar or Maple Syrup
ingredients.name: Agave Nectar

method.description:
Slice the aubergines into batons and whack them in a colander.
Salt and leave for 5 mins to extract the water. After 5 mins, pat
dry and get them onto a baking tray. Drizzle with oil and place in the oven for 30 mins.

While your aubergine is roasting, prep your veg. Thinly slice the
spring onions, red cabbage, garlic, ginger and red chillies.

Grab two bowls. Add the spring onions to one and the red
cabbage to the other. Cover the spring onions with the juice of
the lime, a third of the rice vinegar and a sprinkling of salt.
Over the red cabbage pour another third of the rice vinegar,
half the sesame oil and a sprinkle of salt and mix it
through. Cover and place both in the fridge.

Rinse the sushi rice, then add to a saucepan with just enough
water to cover. Leave for 30 mins to soak.

Heat a drizzle of oil in a wok or large frying
pan and add the garlic, ginger and red chillies and fry over a low
heat. Once softened, add the hoisin sauce, soy sauce, sesame seeds, agave, veggie stock cube, chilli oil and the remaining sesame oil and rice vinegar. Give
everything a good mix together, then add the aubergine straight
from the oven.

Add enough water to just cover the aubergine and leave to
simmer until it has reduced into a lovely thick sauce. Stir often.

Drain the rice and add it to a saucepan with 480ml water (if you're adjusting the servings, use a ratio of one part rice to one and a half parts water) and the shichimi. Bring to the boil, then turn to a simmer, cover
with the lid and simmer until cooked, about 15 mins.

Time to build your lunch boxes. Divide the sushi rice between your Tupperware containers. Add a
generous dollop of aubergine sauce, a helping of marinated red
cabbage and some sliced avocado. Garnish with the spring onions,
the crispy chilli bits from the chilli oil and some more sesame seeds. 
Leave to cool, then box up and pop in the fridge ready for lunchtime. These will keep for up to 3 days in the fridge.

chefs.name: Mob
chefs.summary: Mob is on a mission to instil a love of cooking in everyone, because we believe in the positive impact that it can have on people's lives.
